snp2.5:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying in Gayā at Ṭaṁkitamañca, the haunt of Suciloma [NeedleHair] the yakkha.
snp2.5:1.3: And on that occasion Khara [Rough] the yakkha and Suciloma the yakkha passed by not far from the Blessed One.
snp2.5:1.4: Khara the yakkha said to Suciloma the yakkha,
snp2.5:1.6:
“That’s not a contemplative.
That’s a fake contemplative.
snp2.5:1.7: I’ll find out whether that’s a contemplative or a fake contemplative.”
snp2.5:2.1: So Suciloma the yakkha approached the Blessed One and on arrival leaned his body up against the Blessed One.
snp2.5:2.2: The Blessed One leaned his body away.
snp2.5:2.3: So Suciloma the yakkha said to the Blessed One,
snp2.5:2.5: “No, I’m not afraid of you, friend, just that your touch is evil.”
snp2.5:3.1: “I will ask you a question, contemplative.
snp2.5:3.2:
If you can’t answer me, I will hurl out your mind or rip open your heart or,
grabbing you by the feet, hurl you across the Ganges.”
snp2.5:4.1:
“My friend, I see no one in the cosmos with its devas, Māras,
& Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans,
its royalty & commonfolk, who could hurl out my mind or rip open my heart or,
grabbing me by the feet, hurl me across the Ganges.
snp2.5:4.2: But nevertheless, ask me what you wish.”
snp2.5:4.3: So Suciloma the yakkha addressed the Blessed One in verse:
snp2.5:5.1: Passion & aversion come from what cause?
snp2.5:5.2: Displeasure, delight, horripilation are born from what?
snp2.5:5.3: Arising from what do thoughts fling the mind around,
snp2.5:5.4:
as boys, a (captive) crow?
The Buddha:
snp2.5:6.1: Passion & aversion come from this as a cause;
snp2.5:6.2: displeasure, delight, horripilation are born from this;
snp2.5:6.3: arising from this thoughts fling the mind around,
snp2.5:7.1: They’re born from affection arisen from the mind, from oneself,
snp2.5:7.2: like the trunk-born (shoots) of a banyan tree,
snp2.5:7.4: like a māluva vine spread in a forest.
snp2.5:8.1: Those who discern where it’s born
snp2.5:8.3: They cross over this flood, so hard to cross,
snp2.5:8.4: never crossed before, for the sake of no further becoming.
